extra.message: User input from params['id'][0] is concatenated directly into a SQL query string passed to cursor.execute(). An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL via the 'id' query parameter (e.g. ?id=1 OR 1=1).

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: In AuthBypass.run(), the 'password' parameter is concatenated directly into a SQL query string: "SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='" + username + "' AND password='" + password + "'". Although 'username' has non-word characters stripped, the 'password' value is unsanitized, allowing SQL injection via the password field (e.g., password=' OR '1'='1).

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')

extra.message: In XSSReflected.run(), the 'msg' query parameter is returned directly as HTML content without any encoding or escaping. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript via the 'msg' parameter (e.g. ?msg=<script>alert(1)</script>).

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: In XSSStored.run(), user-supplied 'comment' is stored in the database unescaped (line 88) and then rendered directly into HTML (line 97) without escaping. An attacker can store arbitrary JavaScript that executes for all users who view the guestbook page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: In CommandInjection.run(), the 'domain' query parameter is joined with a shell command and passed to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. An attacker can inject shell commands via the domain parameter (e.g. ?domain=x;id).

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: In VulnHTTPRequestHandler.do_BDR(), the raw HTTP request path (self.path[1:]) is passed directly to subprocess.check_output() with shell=True. An attacker can send an HTTP request with an arbitrary OS command as the path to achieve remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: In UnsafeDeserialization.run(), user-supplied base64 data from the 'object' query parameter is decoded and passed directly to pickle.loads(). Python's pickle module can deserialize arbitrary objects and execute code during deserialization, allowing remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

extra.message: In PathTraversal.run(), the 'path' query parameter is passed to os.path.abspath() and then opened directly without any validation or restriction. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the filesystem using path traversal sequences (e.g. ?path=../../etc/passwd). Additionally, if '://' is in the path, urllib.request.urlopen() is called, enabling SSRF.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: In PathTraversal.run(), when the user-supplied 'path' parameter contains '://', urllib.request.urlopen(path) is called directly with no validation. An attacker can supply arbitrary URLs (e.g. ?path=http://internal-service/) to cause the server to make requests to internal network resources (SSRF).

cwe: CWE-918: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)

extra.message: In UnvalidatedRedirect.run(), the 'path' query parameter is inserted directly into a JavaScript window.location.replace() call without any validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external URLs (e.g. ?path=https://evil.com), enabling phishing attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: In SessionFixation.run(), the 'path' query parameter is inserted directly into a JavaScript window.location call without validation. An attacker can redirect users to arbitrary external URLs while simultaneously fixing their session ID.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')

extra.message: In SessionFixation.run(), the 'session' query parameter is accepted and used to overwrite the user's SESSIONID cookie value. An attacker can pre-set a known session token and then trick the victim into authenticating, after which the attacker uses the known session token to hijack the victim's authenticated session.

cwe: CWE-384: Session Fixation

extra.message: In XSRequestForgery.run(), state-changing operations (updating user email) are performed via HTTP GET requests with no CSRF token validation. An attacker can trick an authenticated user's browser into sending a GET request to /settings?email=attacker@evil.com to silently update the user's email.

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: In Clickjacking.run(), the account deletion action is performed via an HTTP GET request (/danger?delete=1) with no CSRF token. Combined with the missing X-Frame-Options header, an attacker can embed the page in an iframe and trick users into clicking the Delete button (clickjacking/CSRF).

cwe: CWE-352: Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF)

extra.message: In ExecutionAfterRedirect.run(), authentication is checked only by the presence of a cookie, and if no cookie is present a client-side JavaScript redirect is injected. However, the privileged content (Manage Users, Update Database Settings links) is still included in the response body before the redirect executes. A server-side access control check is absent; the redirect is purely client-side and easily bypassed.

cwe: CWE-698: Execution After Redirect (EAR)

extra.message: The SSL context is intentionally configured to re-enable insecure options by unsetting OP_NO_SSLv3 (re-enables SSLv3), OP_NO_COMPRESSION (re-enables TLS compression, enabling CRIME attack), and OP_CIPHER_SERVER_PREFERENCE. These options weaken TLS security significantly.

cwe: CWE-326: Inadequate Encryption Strength

extra.message: The X-XSS-Protection header is explicitly set to '0', disabling the browser's built-in XSS filter. This is intentional in the vulnerable app but removes a defense-in-depth layer.

cwe: CWE-16: Configuration

extra.message: The Content-Security-Policy is set to "default-src * 'unsafe-inline'", which allows inline scripts from any origin. This completely negates CSP as a defense against XSS.

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: HTTP responses never include an X-Frame-Options or frame-ancestors CSP directive. This allows the application to be embedded in iframes, enabling clickjacking attacks (demonstrated by the Clickjacking attack route).

cwe: CWE-1021: Improper Restriction of Rendered UI Layers or Frames

extra.message: Session cookies (SESSIONID) are set without the HttpOnly or Secure flags. Without HttpOnly, client-side JavaScript can read the cookie value, enabling session hijacking via XSS. Without Secure, the cookie is transmitted over unencrypted HTTP connections.

cwe: CWE-1004: Sensitive Cookie Without 'HttpOnly' Flag

extra.message: Hardcoded credentials 'dsvpwa'/'dsvpwa' are checked in AuthBypass.run() and grant access with a default user profile. This backdoor account bypasses normal authentication entirely.

cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

extra.message: Plaintext passwords are stored in db/users.xml, including a privileged admin account password 'Y3RFgwZP$v6v#D'. All user credentials are stored in cleartext in this file which is loaded at server startup.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: In StaticHandler.find(), the URL path is used directly to construct a file path via open('./{}'.format(path)) without any sanitization or restriction to a safe base directory. An attacker can use path traversal sequences in the URL (e.g. /../../../etc/passwd) to read arbitrary files on the system.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
